run.root_cause: The notebook-derived Bayesian prior + embedding probe pipeline is live; next steps are submission bundle parity and calibration.

decision.root_cause: The class-coverage-expansion-probe run (run-0003-0001) achieved val ROC-AUC 0.6652, essentially flat versus the prior baseline (0.6651). The lane leader MLP probe from run-0002-0003 holds 0.6801, which is the best score in branch. The primary bottleneck is the 0.327 train-val gap (0.992 train vs 0.665 val), indicating severe overfitting or domain shift. Padded_cmap at 0.062 confirms weak segment-level localization. Class coverage expansion alone produced no material gain. Five rounds of micro-tuning have been exhausted per the axis-exhaustion negative prior. Before packaging a submission, the higher-value move is to close the gap between the current best (0.6652) and the lane leader (0.6801) by adopting the MLP probe head configuration and then pursuing calibration and domain-shift mitigation.
decision.why: The research verdict says submission-required, but submitting at 0.6652 when the lane leader holds 0.6801 leaves 0.015 AUC on the table. The MLP probe head from run-0002-0003 is a positive prior at 0.68 confidence and has already demonstrated the best score. The negative prior vetoes further micro-tuning on the current head architecture, but does not veto adopting the proven MLP configuration as a baseline. A submission at 0.6801 is materially stronger than one at 0.6652. We should plan a multi-branch follow-up: (1) adopt the MLP probe head as the primary submission candidate, (2) add calibration (temperature scaling) as a parallel branch, and (3) explore domain-shift augmentation as a speculative branch. This preserves branch-search space per the decision rules rather than collapsing to a single calibration tweak.
decision.next_title: MLP probe head adoption + calibration + domain-shift mitigation
decision.portfolio_intent: Close the 0.015 gap between current (0.6652) and lane leader (0.6801) by adopting the proven MLP probe configuration, then calibrate and submit. Open speculative domain-shift branch for upside.

branch_mix.title: MLP probe head adoption from run-0002-0003
branch_mix.hypothesis: Adopting the lane-leader MLP probe head (0.6801) as the primary pipeline configuration will reproduce or exceed that score in the current branch context
branch_mix.rationale: Strongest positive prior; proven score; no architectural risk

branch_mix.title: Temperature scaling calibration on MLP logits
branch_mix.hypothesis: Temperature scaling on the MLP probe head logits will improve ROC-AUC ranking quality by correcting the miscalibrated prior fusion weights
branch_mix.rationale: Low-risk post-hoc calibration; prior fusion barely exceeds raw val (0.662 vs 0.665)

branch_mix.title: Domain-shift augmentation on training soundscapes
branch_mix.hypothesis: Frequency-domain augmentation (specAugment, noise injection) on training soundscapes will narrow the 0.327 train-val gap and improve generalization
branch_mix.rationale: High ceiling but uncertain; train-val gap is the fundamental blocker

problem_frame.focus: MLP probe head adoption and calibration for submission parity
problem_frame.root_cause: Current run (0.6652) is 0.015 below lane leader (0.6801). Train-val gap of 0.327 confirms overfitting/domain shift. Padded_cmap at 0.062 indicates weak segment-level localization. Prior fusion adds negligible lift.

plan.reason: The class-coverage-expansion run (0.6652) is flat versus the prior baseline. The lane leader MLP probe from run-0002-0003 holds 0.6801. The decision directs a multi-branch portfolio: adopt the proven MLP probe head as primary, add temperature-scaling calibration as secondary, and open a speculative domain-shift augmentation branch. No submission packaging is warranted yet — we need to first close the 0.015 gap to the lane leader.
plan.title: MLP probe head adoption from run-0002-0003
plan.hypothesis: Adopting the lane-leader MLP probe head (0.6801) as the primary configuration, then applying temperature scaling calibration, will close the 0.015 AUC gap before submission packaging. A speculative domain-shift augmentation branch may further narrow the 0.327 train-val gap.

problem_frame.title: MLP probe head adoption + calibration + domain-shift mitigation
problem_frame.root_cause: The class-coverage-expansion-probe pipeline achieved val ROC-AUC 0.6652, essentially flat versus the prior run (0.6651). The coverage expansion from 52→75 active classes with 708 cache rows did not materially move the primary metric. The train-val gap (0.992 train vs 0.665 val) confirms overfitting or domain shift. Padded_cmap remains very low (0.062). Prior fusion (0.662) tracks the raw validation score closely, indicating the Bayesian prior adds negligible lift. The lane leader MLP probe from run-0002-0003 holds 0.6801, still the best in branch. The blocking issue is now submission bundle parity and calibration rather than class coverage expansion.
problem_frame.hypothesis: The research verdict says submission-required, but submitting at 0.6652 when the lane leader holds 0.6801 leaves 0.015 AUC on the table. The MLP probe head from run-0002-0003 is a positive prior at 0.68 confidence and has already demonstrated the best score. The negative prior vetoes further micro-tuning on the current head architecture, but does not veto adopting the proven MLP configuration as a baseline. A submission at 0.6801 is materially stronger than one at 0.6652. We should plan a multi-branch follow-up: (1) adopt the MLP probe head as the primary submission candidate, (2) add calibration (temperature scaling) as a parallel branch, and (3) explore domain-shift augmentation as a speculative branch. This preserves branch-search space per the decision rules rather than collapsing to a single calibration tweak.
adopt_now: [require @ 0.88] Use embedding-head style models as complementary signal — card:01-validated-findings-md-perch-and-embedding-findings
[prefer @ 0.68] Adopt MLP probe head from run-0002-0003 as baseline configuration; lane leader at 0.6801 — card:research-run-0002-0003-coverage-mlp-combined-full-75-class-mlp-probe-md-adopt-now
Package current best configuration (run-0003-0001, val ROC-AUC 0.6652) as submission bundle; verdict is submission-required
Calibrate Bayesian prior fusion weights — current prior fusion val metric (0.662) barely exceeds raw val (0.665), indicating miscalibrated temperature or weighting
consider: [conditional @ 0.66] If fitted classes still lag active classes after submission, re-expand coverage before micro-tuning — card:03-next-experiment-priors-md-perch-probe-lane

[conditional @ 0.50] Remaining 8-class gap (67/75 fitted) is still a structural blocker for future iterations — card:research-run-0002-0001-perch-probe-full-class-coverage-expansion-md-root-cause-coverage-gap-narrowed-but-not-closed
Investigate padded_cmap improvement (currently 0.062) as a secondary axis — weak segment-level localization may cap ROC-AUC gains
Explore domain-shift mitigation: augment training soundscapes or apply frequency-domain augmentation to narrow the 0.992→0.665 train-val gap
Temperature scaling or isotonic calibration on the probe head logits before submission to improve ROC-AUC ranking quality
reject: [avoid @ 0.68] Five rounds of micro-tuning (embedding-level mixup, augmentation variants) have regressed from the run-0002 baseline. Three distinct axes are exhausted — card:research-run-0009-0008-perch-probe-round-9-embedding-level-mixup-augmentation-on-run-0008-mlp-head-code-state-md-axis-exhaustion-summary
Do not re-run class coverage expansion in isolation — this run proved it yields flat metric movement (0.6651→0.6652)
Do not pursue further MLP head architecture variants without first establishing submission parity and calibration

candidate_branches.title: Domain-shift augmentation on training soundscapes
candidate_branches.reason_kept: Addresses root cause (0.327 train-val gap); high ceiling; not vetoed by negative priors

candidate_branches.title: Domain-shift augmentation on training soundscapes
candidate_branches.hypothesis: Frequency-domain augmentation (specAugment, noise injection) on training soundscapes will narrow the 0.327 train-val gap and improve generalization to validation soundscapes.
candidate_branches.reason: The 0.327 train-val gap is the fundamental blocker. All other axes have been exhausted. Augmentation directly targets the root cause (overfitting/domain shift) rather than symptoms. Not vetoed by any negative prior.

candidate_branches.title: Temperature scaling calibration on MLP logits
candidate_branches.reason_kept: Low-risk post-hoc calibration targeting miscalibrated prior fusion; not vetoed

candidate_branches.title: Temperature scaling calibration on MLP logits
candidate_branches.hypothesis: Temperature scaling on the MLP probe head logits will improve ROC-AUC ranking quality by correcting the miscalibrated prior fusion weights (val prior fusion 0.662 vs raw val 0.665).
candidate_branches.reason: Low-risk post-hoc calibration; prior fusion adds negligible lift. Temperature scaling is a single-parameter adjustment that cannot regress ranking quality below the uncalibrated baseline.
